metadata.scheme_name: Travel Grant Scheme for College Teachers/Vice Chancellors/Commission Members

raw_data.details: This scheme is implemented by the University Grants Commission for providing financial assistance to permanent teachers of colleges and vice chancellors of state universities, deemed to be universities and central universities, recognised under section 2(f) and 12 (B) of the UGC Act, to present research papers at international conferences abroad. In addition to this, this facility is available to the permanent teachers of institutions of national importance with 10% slots of the total approved cases in a year. Commission members are also eligible to apply under this scheme. Objectives:To enable the permanent Teachers in Colleges / Vice-Chancellors and Commission Members to present their research paper(s) in international conferences.
raw_data.benefits: The financial assistance for permanent teachers of colleges enlisted in [2(f) and 12 B of the UGC Act](https://www.ugc.gov.in/colleges) will be limited to 50% of the total admissible expenditure such as travel, registration fee, per diem allowance and visa fee, once in three years. For Commission members and Vice-Chancellors of Universities enlisted in [2(f) and 12 B of the UGC Act](https://www.ugc.gov.in/colleges), the assistance will be 100% on admissible expenditure once in three years.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant must be a permanent teacher of a college or a Commission Member, or a Vice-Chancellor of a State University, Deemed-to-be University, or Central University recognized under [Sections 2(f) and 12(B) of the UGC Act](https://www.ugc.gov.in/colleges). The applicant must be presenting a research paper at an international conference abroad.
raw_data.application_process: OfflineProcedure to Apply for Assistance:The application should be sent to the Secretary, UGC in the [prescribed application form](https://www.ugc.gov.in/oldpdf/xplanpdf/travelgrant.pdf) along with all necessary enclosures two months prior to the commencement of the conference. Incomplete applications will not be considered and no correspondence will be entertained in this regard. The submission of a proposal does not automatically mean that it has the approval of the UGC. If the paper is co-authored, then a no-objection certificate(s) from the author(s) may be attached with the application. In case of two or more applications for the same paper(s) only one application will be considered and priority will be given to the first author. Conveyance Claim:Persons selected for participation should travel by excursion category tickets in sectors by the cheapest air ticket (in any case not exceeding Air India fare). Actual fare not exceeding AC II Class train fare will be admissible for travel from the college headquarters to the nearest airport and back. Procedure for Approval of the Proposal:The proposal(s) received duly completed in all respects will be evaluated by subject experts. Based on their recommendation, a final decision will be taken by the UGC. The proposals of Vice-Chancellors and Commission Members will be approved by the Chairman, UGC, directly.
raw_data.documents_required: While applyingA copy of the paperAn acceptance letter from the organizers of the ConferenceThe full text of the paper proposed to be presented at the Conference. If the paper is co-authored, then a no-objection certificate(s) from the author(s) may be attached to the application. In case of two or more applications for the same paper(s), only one application will be considered. While submission of the ClaimIn order to ensure prompt action in releasing the grant, the following documents shall be submitted:A statement of account giving details of expenditure incurred on various items viz, travel, air port tax, registration fee, visa fee and daily allowance. A utilization certificate in the prescribed format from the college/university auditor/chartered accountant for the total expenditure incurred on the visit. The details of assistance received or facilities provided by the organisers of the conference or any other similar Indian/foreign agency. The amount made available by the College/University/State Government and other sources. The amount payable for each item by the UGC as per the terms and conditions. A brief note on the participation in the conference. Certificate of participation in the conference. Conversion rate of the US dollar into Indian currency. (From any bank or other financial institution)Note 1: Paging may be done in all the enclosures attached to the claim bill. Note 2: No overwriting/correction may be made in the claim bill & utilization certificate.

To know more please visit Check EligibilityTravel Grant Scheme for College Teachers/Vice Chancellors/Commission MembersMinistry of EducationTravel Grant Scheme for College Teachers/Vice Chancellors/Commission MembersCommission MemberGrantInternational ConferenceTeacherTravelVice-ChancellorCheck EligibilityDetailsThis scheme is implemented by the University Grants Commission for providing financial assistance to permanent teachers of colleges and vice chancellors of state universities, deemed to be universities and central universities, recognised under section 2(f) and 12 (B) of the UGC Act, to present research papers at international conferences abroad. In addition to this, this facility is available to the permanent teachers of institutions of national importance with 10% slots of the total approved cases in a year. Commission members are also eligible to apply under this scheme. Objectives:To enable the permanent Teachers in Colleges / Vice-Chancellors and Commission Members to present their research paper(s) in international conferences. BenefitsThe financial assistance for permanent teachers of colleges enlisted in [2(f) and 12 B of the UGC Act](https://www.ugc.gov.in/colleges) will be limited to 50% of the total admissible expenditure such as travel, registration fee, per diem allowance and visa fee, once in three years. For Commission members and Vice-Chancellors of Universities enlisted in [2(f) and 12 B of the UGC Act](https://www.ugc.gov.in/colleges), the assistance will be 100% on admissible expenditure once in three years. EligibilityThe applicant must be a permanent teacher of a college or a Commission Member, or a Vice-Chancellor of a State University, Deemed-to-be University, or Central University recognized under [Sections 2(f) and 12(B) of the UGC Act](https://www.ugc.gov.in/colleges). The applicant must be presenting a research paper at an international conference abroad. Application ProcessOfflineProcedure to Apply for Assistance:The application should be sent to the Secretary, UGC in the [prescribed application form](https://www.ugc.gov.in/oldpdf/xplanpdf/travelgrant.pdf) along with all necessary enclosures two months prior to the commencement of the conference. Incomplete applications will not be considered and no correspondence will be entertained in this regard. The submission of a proposal does not automatically mean that it has the approval of the UGC. If the paper is co-authored, then a no-objection certificate(s) from the author(s) may be attached with the application. In case of two or more applications for the same paper(s) only one application will be considered and priority will be given to the first author. Conveyance Claim:Persons selected for participation should travel by excursion category tickets in sectors by the cheapest air ticket (in any case not exceeding Air India fare). Actual fare not exceeding AC II Class train fare will be admissible for travel from the college headquarters to the nearest airport and back. Procedure for Approval of the Proposal:The proposal(s) received duly completed in all respects will be evaluated by subject experts. Based on their recommendation, a final decision will be taken by the UGC. The proposals of Vice-Chancellors and Commission Members will be approved by the Chairman, UGC, directly. Documents RequiredWhile applyingA copy of the paperAn acceptance letter from the organizers of the ConferenceThe full text of the paper proposed to be presented at the Conference. If the paper is co-authored, then a no-objection certificate(s) from the author(s) may be attached to the application. In case of two or more applications for the same paper(s), only one application will be considered. While submission of the ClaimIn order to ensure prompt action in releasing the grant, the following documents shall be submitted:A statement of account giving details of expenditure incurred on various items viz, travel, air port tax, registration fee, visa fee and daily allowance. A utilization certificate in the prescribed format from the college/university auditor/chartered accountant for the total expenditure incurred on the visit. The details of assistance received or facilities provided by the organisers of the conference or any other similar Indian/foreign agency. The amount made available by the College/University/State Government and other sources. The amount payable for each item by the UGC as per the terms and conditions. A brief note on the participation in the conference. Certificate of participation in the conference. Conversion rate of the US dollar into Indian currency. (From any bank or other financial institution)Note 1: Paging may be done in all the enclosures attached to the claim bill. Note 2: No overwriting/correction may be made in the claim bill & utilization certificate. Frequently Asked QuestionsWho is eligible to apply under this scheme? Eligible applicants include:Permanent college teachersVice-Chancellors of universitiesUGC Commission MembersWhich institutions are covered under the scheme? Institutions recognized under Sections 2(f) and 12(B) of the UGC Act 1956 are covered. How often can an applicant avail this grant? An applicant can avail the grant once every three years. What financial assistance is provided to college teachers? Permanent teachers receive up to 50% of admissible expenses. What financial assistance is provided to Vice-Chancellors and Commission Members? They receive 100% of admissible expenses. What expenses are covered under the scheme? The scheme covers:Air travelRegistration feeVisa feePer diem allowanceAirport taxesIs there any limitation on airfare reimbursement? Yes, airfare is limited to the cheapest available fare, not exceeding the fare of Air India. How can one apply for the scheme? Applicants must submit a prescribed application form to the UGC Secretary along with required documents. What is the deadline for submitting applications? Applications must be submitted at least two months before the conference. Sources And References[Guidelines](https://www.ugc.gov.in/oldpdf/xplanpdf/travelgrant.pdf)

raw_data.scheme_name: Travel Grant Scheme for College Teachers/Vice Chancellors/Commission Members

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Eligible applicants include:Permanent college teachersVice-Chancellors of universitiesUGC Commission Members

faqs.question: Which institutions are covered under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Institutions recognized under Sections 2(f) and 12(B) of the UGC Act 1956 are covered.

faqs.question: How often can an applicant avail this grant?
faqs.answer: An applicant can avail the grant once every three years.

faqs.question: What financial assistance is provided to college teachers?
faqs.answer: Permanent teachers receive up to 50% of admissible expenses.

faqs.question: What financial assistance is provided to Vice-Chancellors and Commission Members?
What expenses are covered under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme covers:Air travelRegistration feeVisa feePer diem allowanceAirport taxes

faqs.question: Is there any limitation on airfare reimbursement?
faqs.answer: Yes, airfare is limited to the cheapest available fare, not exceeding the fare of Air India.

faqs.question: How can one apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants must submit a prescribed application form to the UGC Secretary along with required documents.

faqs.question: What is the deadline for submitting applications?
faqs.answer: Applications must be submitted at least two months before the conference.
